center.name: 普图马约省
center.summary: 普图马约省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括厄瓜多尔斑尾蜂鸟、Purplish jacamar、Olive-striped flycatcher等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies occurs in the coastal mountains of Aragua in northern Venezuela, the Andes of western Venezuela, in the Serranía del Perijá along the Colombia-Venezuela border and in the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta of northern Colombia, and from the eastern Andes in Colombia south to Valle, Putumayo, Nariño and Pichincha Province in Ecuador

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: napoensis is found in southern Colombia's Putumayo Department and in Ecuador's Sucumbíos and Napo provinces

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: obscura is found in the Eastern Andes of southern Colombia in the departments of Caquetá and Putumayo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in southern Colombia's departments of Caquetá, Putumayo, and Amazonas; in the eastern half of Ecuador; and in northern Peru south at least to the Río Marañón and perhaps further to the rios Ucayali and Cushabatay. Though some maps show its range extending into far western Brazil, the SACC has no records from that country.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: A separate population is found in southern Colombia's Putumayo and Amazonas departments and possibly slightly into northeastern Ecuador

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: fasciaticollis: from far southern Colombia's Putumayo Department south on the eastern slope of the Andes through Ecuador and Peru into extreme northwestern Bolivia The olive-striped flycatcher inhabits the interior and edges of humid forest, secondary forest, and plantations in the foothills and subtropical zone

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: spectabilis: from far southern Colombia's Putumayo Department south through eastern Ecuador into northern Peru as far as Huánuco Department The orange-billed sparrow primarily inhabits the understory of humid primary and secondary evergreen forest in the tropical zone.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The purplish jacamar is found in the western Amazon Basin from southeast Colombia's Putumayo and Amazonas Departments south through eastern Ecuador into eastern Peru and east into Brazil as far as the Juruá River in Amazonas state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in south-central Colombia's Meta Department and from Putumayo Department in southern Colombia into eastern Ecuador as far as Pastaza Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: caquetae is found adjoining the nominate's range in the southern Colombian departments of Putumayo and Caquetá

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Ecuadorian piedtail is found in the eastern foothills of the Andes from Colombia's Putumayo and Cauca departments south through eastern Ecuador into northeastern Peru's departments of San Martín and Loreto

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The orange-fronted plushcrown is found in southeastern Colombia's Putumayo and Amazonas departments, eastern Ecuador and Peru, Beni Department in northeastern Bolivia, and from them into western Brazil east to the lower Juruá and upper Purus rivers
